entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: The agricultural crop being studied for its blanchability.

entities.name: BLANCHABILITY
entities.description: The capacity of a peanut genotype to release its testa or seed coat.

entities.name: GENOTYPE
entities.description: The genetic constitution of an individual organism.

entities.name: SEED QUALITY
entities.description: A characteristic of the seed that influences its blanchability.

entities.name: HARVEST DATE
entities.description: The timing of harvest which affects the blanchability of the peanut.

entities.name: LEVEL OF MATURITY
entities.description: The stage of development of the peanut seed influencing blanchability.

entities.name: POST-HARVEST STORAGE PERIOD
entities.description: The duration and conditions of storage after harvest affecting blanchability.

entities.name: TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The thermal condition during post-harvest storage influencing blanchability.

entities.name: BREEDING
entities.description: The science of changing the traits of plants to produce desired characteristics.

entities.name: SELECTION
entities.description: The process of choosing specific genotypes for breeding based on desired traits.

entities.name: BLANCHING PERCENTAGE
entities.description: A quantitative measure of the extent to which a peanut genotype is blanchable.

entities.name: GENOTYPE X ENVIRONMENT INTERACTION
entities.description: The interaction between the genetic makeup of a plant and its environment.

entities.name: MARKER-ASSISTED SELECTION
entities.description: A breeding method that uses genetic markers to select for specific traits.

entities.name: BLANCHING
entities.description: The process of removing the testa or seed coat from peanuts.
